headline: TRY is lagging peers, but policy and reserves still cushion the read while global feeds remain incomplete.

briefing.caveat_message: Global rates and dollar feeds were incomplete, so the external macro read is less certain than the market and domestic read.

summary.market_message: USD/TRY is +1.19% over 20 sessions, with TRY vs peers at +6.03%.

why_read.label: Pressure
why_read.title: Spot pressure is still live
why_read.detail: TRY is +6.03% weaker than the peer basket over 20 sessions, while USD/TRY is +1.19% over the same window.

why_read.label: Support
why_read.title: Domestic cushion is still there
why_read.detail: CBRT policy rate is 37.00%, and official reserves are +4.06% over the latest reserve window.

why_read.label: Unclear
why_read.title: External macro is still cloudy
why_read.detail: Global rates and dollar feeds were incomplete, so the external macro read is less certain than the market and domestic read.

trigger_cards.title: TRY vs peers
trigger_cards.detail: If TRY keeps lagging the peer basket beyond the current +6.03% gap, the read should move more bearish.

trigger_cards.title: Reserve trend
trigger_cards.detail: If reserve momentum rolls over from the current supportive trend, the domestic cushion gets thinner.

trigger_cards.title: Global macro clarity
trigger_cards.detail: If the missing global-rates and dollar feeds come back showing stronger USD pressure, the current neutral external read should move higher.
trigger_cards.now: Now external macro read is neutralized by missing feeds
